data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp employs a microservices architecture to efficiently handle its large-scale, global user base.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services using RESTful APIs or gRPC for communication.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Messages are encrypted from sender to recipient, ensuring privacy and security.
data.technical_spec: Uses the Signal Protocol for encryption.

data.title: Global Edge Network
data.description: Distributed server infrastructure to minimize latency and enhance performance.
data.technical_spec: Content Delivery Networks (CDN) and regional balancing.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: WhatsApp's infrastructure is designed to scale horizontally to accommodate thousands of messages per second.
data.technical_spec: Auto-scaling Kubernetes clusters and load balancing.

data.title: Cross-Platform Compatibility
data.description: Supports multiple operating systems, ensuring high accessibility.
data.technical_spec: Native applications for iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS.
